title: The Evolution of Nucleic Acid Discrimination: Data and Code
description: Computational analysis showing that ancient protein domains in LUCA's proteome exhibit widespread weak RNA/DNA discrimination in structural-contact analyses, consistent with ancestral generalism. Includes annotation-based census of LUCA's NA-binding proteome (Wehbi x RBPWorld Pfam intersection), NAS-Bench structural specificity analysis of 18 independent domain families (19 rows; PF07521 and PF10996 share source protein 9BCU), and ancestral state reconstruction with convergence analysis. AF3/Boltz-2 structural predictions (Phase 3) are planned but not yet completed.
notes: This dataset accompanies the manuscript 'The Evolution of Nucleic Acid Discrimination'. AF3/Boltz-2 structural predictions (Phase 3) are planned but not yet completed; pilot feasibility scripts are included.
